summary: Grounded the agent-surface discussion in the current Launchdeck repository and current official host specifications. The repo already ships a canonical cross-host Skill installer and stable JSON lifecycle contract, but has no MCP server or plugin package yet.

Handoff assessment is ready-for-contract. The current Launchdeck repository is the locked implementation target; first release is one shared Kernel with backward-compatible CLI, low-risk local stdio MCP, one canonical Skill, and separately generated Codex/Claude Plugins. Hard unknowns and conflicts are zero; host elicitation and path-variable verification remain non-blocking soft unknowns.
confirmed_decisions: Lock F:/github/launchdeck as the implementation target and current behavior source of truth.
Lock first release to low-risk local stdio MCP, existing configured projects, canonical Skill, and generated Codex/Claude Plugins.
Defer automatic adoption writes, medium-plus MCP risk, force-stop, risky clean/reset, remote MCP, hooks, UI, native binaries, and enterprise policy implementation.
